name: wat321
description: Willy's AI Tools - Real-time Claude & Codex usage widgets

wat321.enableClaude.markdownDescription: Enable Claude Usage and Claude Session Token widgets. Widgets are hidden automatically if Claude CLI is not installed. Changes apply immediately.

wat321.experimental.forceClaudeAutoCompa.markdownDescription: **Experimental.** Arms Claude's Auto-Compact for your next message prompt to Claude. Triggers Auto-Compact on next prompt. Disarms after 30 seconds of no activity. Produces a higher-quality compaction result than the manual `/compact` command, preserving tool results and reasoning. This is the only WAT321 tool that writes outside `~/.wat321/`. Your Claude settings are backed up automatically each time the tool is armed. A confirmation dialog will appear before arming. *Useful for long sessions when performance degrades.*

wat321.enableCodex.markdownDescription: Enable Codex Usage and Codex Session Token widgets. Widgets are hidden automatically if Codex CLI is not installed. Changes apply immediately.

enumDescriptions: Automatic - Full when only one provider is active, Compact when both are active
Full progress bars (10 blocks) with all details
Compact progress bars (5 blocks) for usage, text-only for session tokens
Text-only view, progress bars remain in tooltips on hover
wat321.displayMode.markdownDescription: Status bar display density. Auto adjusts based on how many providers are active. Changes apply immediately.

wat321.statusBarPriority.markdownDescription: Base priority for WAT321 status bar items. Higher values appear further left. Adjust if widgets overlap with other extensions. *Requires window reload.*

wat321.clearAllData.markdownDescription: **Reset WAT321** - Restore WAT321 to its default state and clear its saved local data. This resets all WAT321 settings and clears anything WAT321 has stored locally, so everything rebuilds cleanly on the next refresh. If any WAT321 tool appears unresponsive, this will reset every tool to a known-good state. A confirmation dialog will appear before anything is changed.

scripts.build: npm run clean && npm run lint && tsc
scripts.package: npm run build && npx vsce package
